title: Houses for Rent in Brooklyn, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Brooklyn, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Brooklyn, Ohio? See its location southwest of Cleveland, rent benchmarks, and how to tell us what you need in the area.
answerBox: Brooklyn, Ohio is a small Cuyahoga County suburb immediately southwest of Cleveland — distinct from Cleveland's own Old Brooklyn and Brooklyn Centre neighborhoods. Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't currently manage homes in the suburb of Brooklyn itself, but we do manage voucher-friendly homes nearby in Cleveland's 44109 area. Tell us what you need and we'll match you to what's available.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Brooklyn, Ohio
body: The City of Brooklyn is a Cuyahoga County suburb directly southwest of Cleveland, bordering the city rather than sitting inside it. It's easy to confuse with Cleveland's own **Old Brooklyn** and **Brooklyn Centre** neighborhoods, which are separate city SPAs (Statistical Planning Areas) a short distance away — Brooklyn the suburb is its own municipality with its own local government, distinct from those Cleveland neighborhood names.

**Rent Finder Cleveland manages 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, plus a smaller presence on the city's west/southwest side — the suburb of Brooklyn itself isn't part of our current portfolio.** If a Brooklyn address specifically is what you need, a suburb-focused property manager will have more direct listings there. If you're flexible on the exact city line, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we can point you toward homes we manage in neighboring Cleveland areas, including Old Brooklyn and Brooklyn Centre just across the border.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house near Brooklyn, Ohio?
body: Rents in the immediate Brooklyn/southwest Cleveland corridor vary by house size and condition, but as a Greater Cleveland-area benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a metro median of about **$1,100** for a 2-bedroom and **$1,350** for a 3-bedroom home, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report — which weights toward larger managed buildings — shows higher averages across the board. Within our own portfolio, 2-bedroom rental homes typically run **$750–$1,100** a month and 3-bedroom homes typically run **$1,000–$1,500**, based on our real current Cleveland-area listings.

sections.h2: Getting around Brooklyn and southwest Cleveland
body: Brooklyn sits just southwest of Cleveland, near the city's Old Brooklyn and Brooklyn Centre neighborhoods, which are served by Greater Cleveland RTA bus routes into downtown and sit near I-71 and I-480 for drivers. Old Brooklyn is also near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo, one of the area's well-known public landmarks, and the broader area is within a short drive of Steelyard Commons and the West 25th Street corridor.

items: **Location:** Cuyahoga County suburb directly southwest of Cleveland
**Not the same as:** Cleveland's Old Brooklyn and Brooklyn Centre neighborhoods (ZIP 44109 area)
**Transit nearby:** RTA bus routes serving southwest Cleveland; I-71 and I-480 access
**Nearby landmark:** Cleveland Metroparks Zoo, adjacent to Old Brooklyn

body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, so acceptance in the suburb of Brooklyn depends on the individual owner rather than a blanket local rule. **Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, including our homes in Cleveland's nearby 44109 area (Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre). The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the Housing Choice Voucher program countywide, including both Cleveland and its suburbs, and accepts preliminary applications year-round. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for more on the local process.

sections.h2: Looking beyond the suburb of Brooklyn
body: If your search includes Cleveland proper, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the neighborhoods where we manage inventory, including [Old Brooklyn](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/) just across the city line from the suburb of Brooklyn. You can also browse our full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) to compare nearby communities.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses in Brooklyn, Ohio?
faq.a: Not in the suburb of Brooklyn itself. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a smaller presence in the city's southwest, including the nearby Old Brooklyn and Brooklyn Centre neighborhoods. Tell us what you're looking for and we'll point you to comparable homes we do manage.

faq.q: Is Brooklyn, Ohio the same as Cleveland's Old Brooklyn neighborhood?
faq.a: No. Brooklyn is its own Cuyahoga County suburb directly southwest of Cleveland with its own city government. Old Brooklyn and Brooklyn Centre are separate Cleveland neighborhoods nearby. The similar names are a common source of confusion for renters searching online.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Brooklyn, Ohio?
faq.a: In our own nearby Cleveland-area portfolio, 2-bedroom rental homes typically run $750–$1,100 a month and 3-bedroom homes run $1,000–$1,500. As a broader metro benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units.

faq.q: Do landlords near Brooklyn, Ohio accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages, including those in the nearby Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre area, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. CMHA administers the voucher program for all of Cuyahoga County.
